question: The concept of 'supply chain management'
options: Cost advantages from producing a variety of products together
Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
Technical, purchasing, financial, managerial and risk-bearing
Coordinating the flow of goods, information and money from supplier to customer
answer: Coordinating the flow of goods, information and money from supplier to customer

question: 'Just-in-time' (JIT) production
options: Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
Receives materials exactly when needed, reducing inventory costs
QC checks finished products; QA monitors the entire production process
Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time
answer: Receives materials exactly when needed, reducing inventory costs

question: The concept of 'economies of scope'
options: Cost advantages from producing a variety of products together
Contracting external providers to perform non-core business functions
The percentage of total productive capacity currently being used
Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time
answer: Cost advantages from producing a variety of products together

question: 'Batch production' involves
options: Greater control over supply chain, quality and costs
Producing a set quantity of identical items before changing to a different product
Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements
Continuous improvement involving all employees to achieve customer satisfaction
answer: Producing a set quantity of identical items before changing to a different product

question: The concept of 'job production'
options: Cost advantages from producing a variety of products together
Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time
Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements
The percentage of total productive capacity currently being used
answer: Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements

question: 'Flow production' (mass production)
options: Contracting external providers to perform non-core business functions
Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time
Cost advantages from producing a variety of products together
Continuous production of large quantities on an assembly line
answer: Continuous production of large quantities on an assembly line

question: The concept of 'total quality management' (TQM)
options: Contracting external providers to perform non-core business functions
Technical, purchasing, financial, managerial and risk-bearing
Continuous improvement involving all employees to achieve customer satisfaction
Producing a set quantity of identical items before changing to a different product
answer: Continuous improvement involving all employees to achieve customer satisfaction

question: 'Quality control' differs from 'quality assurance' because
options: QC checks finished products; QA monitors the entire production process
Producing a set quantity of identical items before changing to a different product
Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time
answer: QC checks finished products; QA monitors the entire production process

question: The concept of 'critical path analysis'
options: Producing a set quantity of identical items before changing to a different product
Cost advantages from producing a variety of products together
Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time
Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements
answer: Identifies the longest sequence of dependent tasks to determine minimum project time

question: 'Benchmarking' in business involves
options: Continuous production of large quantities on an assembly line
Coordinating the flow of goods, information and money from supplier to customer
Continuous small improvements involving all employees
Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
answer: Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements

question: The concept of 'outsourcing'
options: Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements
The percentage of total productive capacity currently being used
Contracting external providers to perform non-core business functions
QC checks finished products; QA monitors the entire production process
answer: Contracting external providers to perform non-core business functions

question: 'Vertical integration' benefits include
options: The percentage of total productive capacity currently being used
Greater control over supply chain, quality and costs
Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
Continuous small improvements involving all employees
answer: Greater control over supply chain, quality and costs

question: The concept of 'economies of scale' types include
options: Coordinating the flow of goods, information and money from supplier to customer
Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements
Technical, purchasing, financial, managerial and risk-bearing
Greater control over supply chain, quality and costs
answer: Technical, purchasing, financial, managerial and risk-bearing

question: 'Kaizen' philosophy promotes
options: Continuous small improvements involving all employees
Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
Technical, purchasing, financial, managerial and risk-bearing
Producing a set quantity of identical items before changing to a different product
answer: Continuous small improvements involving all employees

question: The concept of 'capacity utilisation'
options: The percentage of total productive capacity currently being used
Comparing performance against industry best practice to identify improvements
Contracting external providers to perform non-core business functions
Making one unique product at a time to specific customer requirements
answer: The percentage of total productive capacity currently being used
